indigo-ink.$description: Indigo Ink — Violet action color for filled buttons, selected navigation states, and focused conversion moments.

indigo-hover.$description: Indigo Hover — Violet text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases. Do not promote it to the primary CTA color

midnight-ink.$description: Midnight Ink — Primary heading and body text — deep near-black with a cool blue undertone that anchors the entire type system

slate.$description: Slate — Gray text accent for links, tags, and emphasized short phrases.

steel.$description: Steel — Tertiary body text and helper copy — sits between slate and the muted violet tints used on sub-labels

smoke.$description: Smoke — Muted violet-tinted text for large decorative headings and supporting copy — never for body paragraphs

pure-white.$description: Pure White — Page canvas and elevated card surfaces; the default background against which everything else is measured

mist.$description: Mist — Footer background and section banding — a barely-perceptible cool tint that separates regions without a visible line

frost.$description: Frost — Primary border color, subtle surface tint, and button hover backgrounds; the workhorse neutral that divides content

lavender-border.$description: Lavender Border — Hairline outline button border (1px) — the violet companion to indigo fills; pairs with white text for ghost CTAs

lilac-border.$description: Lilac Border — Secondary outline button border and softer dividers; a paler sibling of lavender for tertiary ghost actions

periwinkle-wash.$description: Periwinkle Wash — Lightest violet surface — soft tinted backgrounds for highlighted cards, tag pills, and subtle emphasis blocks

deep-violet.$description: Deep Violet — Heaviest accent stroke — rare use for emphasized borders or graphic frames; reads almost as a navy

amethyst-edge.$description: Amethyst Edge — Mid-violet outline border for developer-facing buttons; sits between indigo ink and lavender border

sohne-var.$description: Sole type family across the entire interface — headings, body, navigation, buttons, and labels. Weight 300 dominates even at 56px display size, which is the signature: headlines whisper instead of shout, earning authority through restraint rather than volume. Weight 400 appears only at 14–16px for navigation and button text where small-size readability needs the extra stroke.

canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Primary page background for hero and most content sections

band.$description: Surface level 1: Footer band and quiet section separators; a barely-there cool wash

frosted-card.$description: Surface level 2: Borders, dividers, hover backgrounds; creates depth without shadow

lavender-card.$description: Surface level 3: Tinted surface for highlighted content blocks and emphasized cards

indigo-surface.$description: Surface level 4: Brand accent surface for dark-mode feature cards or dramatic highlight panels
